title: A PQC Migration CBOM is not an Inventory CBOM
why:
- A migration-oriented Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) goes beyond inventory by recording where cryptography is applied, how it is configured at runtime, and what dependencies and trust boundaries exist.
- A migration CBOM tracks what must change, who needs to coordinate, and what is blocking activation of quantum-safe modes, making it crucial for managing PQC (post-quantum cryptography) migration.
- The migration CBOM exposes full ecosystem dependency chains, including vendor, protocol, and partner readiness for quantum-safe capabilities.
- Unlike an inventory CBOM, the migration CBOM serves as a living, versioned roadmap with vendor availability dates, hardware refresh cycles, and certification timelines, guiding actionable PQC migration plans.
- Structured, machine-readable migration CBOM profiles are the foundation for automated readiness scoring, gap analysis, and policy enforcement during PQC transitions.
- Standardized migration CBOMs provide a shared language for coordination among procurement, security architecture, vendors, and regulators in quantum-safe migration projects.
